text: Sliding Window Pattern: Fixed-size window maximum/minimum. Use deque (monotonic queue). Window of size k. Add elements to deque from right (maintain decreasing order for max, increasing for min). Remove elements outside window from left. Front of deque = max/min in current window. Time: O(n), Space: O(k). Use for: sliding window maximum, moving average.

Sliding Window Pattern: Variable-size window with sum constraint. Find smallest subarray with sum >= target. Expand right, accumulate sum. While sum >= target: update answer with current window size, shrink from left. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). Template: right expands to satisfy, left shrinks while still satisfying. Track best result during shrink phase.

Sliding Window Pattern: Longest substring with at most K distinct characters. Expand right, add char to frequency map. If distinct count > k: shrink from left until distinct count <= k. Track max window size. Time: O(n), Space: O(k). Same template for: at most k zeros, at most k replacements, exactly k (equals at-most-k minus at-most-(k-1)).

Sliding Window Pattern: Minimum window substring. Find smallest window in s containing all chars of t. Use need map (char frequencies in t) and have counter. Expand right: when char in need and freq matches, increment have. When have == len(need): try shrinking from left. Time: O(m+n), Space: O(m+n). Classic hard sliding window problem.

Sliding Window Pattern: Longest repeating character replacement. With at most k replacements, find longest substring with one repeated char. Track max frequency of any char in window. Window is valid if window_size - max_freq <= k. Expand right always; shrink left only when invalid. Time: O(n), max_freq is tricky — only update up, never shrink (valid optimization).

Sliding Window Pattern: Permutation in string (anagram check). Fixed window of size len(p). Use frequency arrays of size 26. Check if window has same freq as pattern. Slide window: add right char, remove left char, compare freq arrays. Or count 'matches' (positions where freq matches). Time: O(n), Space: O(1) for fixed alphabet.

Sliding Window Pattern: Maximum consecutive ones with K flips. Find longest subarray with at most k zeros (flipped to ones). Variable window. Expand right. Count zeros in window. If zeros > k: shrink from left until zeros <= k. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). Variation: flips have cooldown or positions of zeros tracked for O(n) with deque.

Sliding Window Pattern: Subarray sum equals K (with prefix sum). Sliding window doesn't work with negatives. Use prefix sum + hash map instead. ps[i] = sum of first i elements. Count subarrays summing to k: for each i, answer += freq[ps[i] - k]. Update freq[ps[i]]++. Time: O(n), Space: O(n). For non-negative only: two pointers work in O(n).

Sliding Window Pattern: Fruit into baskets / at most 2 types. Maximum subarray containing at most 2 distinct values. Variable window with frequency map. When distinct types > 2: shrink from left, remove char if freq drops to 0. Track max window size. Time: O(n). General form: at most k distinct elements — replace 2 with k.

Sliding Window Pattern: Sliding window with deque for k-size window sum. Compute sum of all windows of size k. Simple approach: prefix sum, window sum = ps[i+k] - ps[i]. Time: O(n). For running computation without prefix array: maintain running sum, subtract outgoing element and add incoming at each step. Time: O(n), Space: O(1).

Sliding Window Pattern: Longest substring without repeating characters. Variable window. Expand right, track last seen index of each char in hash map. If char seen and last index >= left: move left to last_seen[char]+1. Update last_seen. Track max window size. Time: O(n), Space: O(charset). Template: use index map instead of frequency for O(1) shrink.

Sliding Window Pattern: Number of substrings containing all three characters. Count substrings of s containing a, b, c at least once. Two approaches: slide window tracking last position of each char; min(last_a, last_b, last_c)+1 gives count of valid substrings ending at current position. Sum all. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). Complementary counting approach.

Sliding Window Pattern: Maximum sum of subarray of size K. Fixed window. Initialize with first k elements sum. Slide: add next, remove first. Track maximum. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). Foundation: Kadane's algorithm is dynamic version (variable window for max subarray without fixed size constraint). Max subarray: dp[i] = max(nums[i], dp[i-1]+nums[i]).

Sliding Window Pattern: Count subarrays with product less than K. Expand right, multiply into product. While product >= k: divide out left element, advance left. Subarrays ending at right with product < k: right - left + 1. Accumulate count. Time: O(n). Works for positive numbers only (dividing brings product down predictably).

Sliding Window Pattern: Sliding window median. Fixed window, find median. Use two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half. Balance heaps when window slides: remove outgoing element (lazy deletion with hash map), add incoming element, rebalance. Median = max of lower heap (odd) or average of both tops (even). Time: O(n log k).

Sliding Window Pattern: Grumpy bookstore owner. Fixed window of size k (minutes using secret technique). Find window maximizing sum of normally-grumpy customers (grumpy[i]==1) + always-satisfied customers. Precompute base (all satisfied customers). Maximize additional customers from window of grumpy minutes. Time: O(n).

Sliding Window Pattern: Longest subarray with ones after deleting one element. Delete exactly one element; find longest subarray of 1s. Equivalent: longest subarray with at most one 0. Variable window with count of zeros <= 1. When zeros > 1: shrink from left. Final answer: max_window - 1 (for deleted element). Time: O(n).

Sliding Window Pattern: Maximum points obtainable from cards. Take k cards from either end of row. Complement: minimum sum window of size (n-k) in middle. Total - min middle sum = max points. Fixed window of size n-k sliding through middle of array. Time: O(n). Insight: instead of choosing from ends, find what NOT to choose in middle.

Sliding Window Pattern: DNA sequence — all repeated substrings of length L. Find all substrings of length L appearing more than once. Use rolling hash or slide window with hash set. Add current window hash; if already seen, add to result. Slide: remove leftmost char contribution, add rightmost char. Rabin-Karp rolling hash for O(1) slide. Time: O(n), Space: O(n).

Sliding Window Pattern: When to use sliding window. Use when: problem involves contiguous subarray or substring, looking for optimal window size (max/min/count), constraint is monotonic (if window is valid, smaller windows might also be valid, or adding elements can only worsen/improve). Invalid for: non-contiguous subsets, negative numbers with sum constraints, non-monotonic validity.
